perturbation.original_text: Franchisor desires to appoint Franchisee, and Franchisee desires to be so appointed, to act as Franchisor's exclusive Franchisee in the territories of North America and South America ("Territory") in the manner set out in this Agreement, for setting up, managing and operating entertainment centres and gaming arcades involving virtual reality, sports simulation technology and proprietary gamification technologies to provide sport and recreational activities/services to the public ("Smaaash Centres"), upon the terms and subject to the conditions hereinafter provided.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisor desires to appoint Franchisee, and Franchisee desires to be so appointed, to act as Franchisor's exclusive Licensee in the territories of North America and South America ("Territory") in the manner set out in this Agreement, for setting up, managing and operating entertainment centres and gaming arcades involving virtual reality, sports simulation technology and proprietary gamification technologies to provide sport and recreational activities/services to the public ("Smaaash Centres"), upon the terms and subject to the conditions hereinafter provided.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Franchisee' to 'Licensee' in the recitals creates confusion about the actual relationship between the parties. Throughout the agreement, the term 'Franchisee' is used. Introducing 'Licensee' makes it ambiguous and contradicts the specific grant of a 'franchise' as defined later in the document, leading to uncertainty about the intended obligations and rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights granted to the Franchisee. Subject to Section 1.2, Franchisor hereby grants to Franchisee the exclusive right, (a) to establish and operate Smaaash Centres in the Territory, (b) to sub-license the right to establish and operate Smaaash Centres to third party franchisees in and for the Territory, (c) a license to use the products and other services developed by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres (including a right to authorise the use of products and services developed by the Franchisor by third party franchisees), in the Territory, and (d) to identify third party franchisees for the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. The rights granted herein include the limited license to use the Trademarks of the Franchisor (the details of which are morefully set out in Exhibit A), as set out in Section 3 of this Agreement, for the purposes of establishing and operating the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. It is clarified that if third party franchisees shall be establishing and operating Smaaash Centres in the manner contemplated under this Agreement, then the Franchisee shall ensure that such third party franchisees complies with all the obligations and duties of the Franchisee, as recorded under this Agreement, and to this extent, Franchisee may enter into relevant agreements with such third party franchisees.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Franchisee' to 'Licensee' in the initial recital introduces a significant contradiction. The agreement consistently refers to 'Franchisee' and grants specific rights to the 'Franchisee,' such as the exclusive right to establish and operate 'Smaaash Centres' and sub-license these rights. By using 'Licensee' in the initial recital, the modification creates ambiguity about the true nature of the relationship. This directly contradicts the explicit grant of rights to the 'Franchisee' in the 'Rights granted to the Franchisee' section, leading to uncertainty about whether the licensee also possesses these franchisee-specific rights, or if the agreement applies to the Licensee at all.

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Franchisee' to 'Customer' introduces ambiguity as to who is responsible for maintaining customer relations. Throughout the document, 'Franchisee' has specific obligations. Replacing it with 'Customer' in this isolated instance implies that the end consumer or a different entity is responsible, which creates conflict with the rest of the agreement. Making the contradiction in the first contract year will cause the conflict to appear immediately.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Best Efforts. (a) Franchisee shall exercise its best efforts to effectively market, promote, and publicise the Smaaash Centres throughout the Territory. Franchisee shall also be obligated to identify suitable locations to set up the Smaaash Centres. Franchisee shall comply with, and ensure that the third party sub-franchisees comply with the standards prescribed by Franchisor (as provided in the operating manuals which shall be shared by Franchisor with Franchisee) with respect to the services, products and operations of the Smaaash Centres and shall operate the Smaaash Centres in strict conformity with such standards and specifications as Franchisor may from time to time prescribe to Franchisee. Franchisee shall refrain from deviating from such standards and specifications without Franchisor's prior written consent and from otherwise operating in any manner which reflects adversely on the Trademarks and Smaaash Centres.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly places the responsibility of maintaining customer relations on the 'Franchisee.' By changing 'Franchisee' to 'Customer', the altered text creates ambiguity about who bears this responsibility. The contradicted text explicitly states that the 'Franchisee' must market, promote and publicise the Smaaash Centers. The change causes a contradiction because previously the Franchisee had obligations which are now uncertain as the term has been switched to Customer. This undermines the enforceability and understanding of the agreement, leading to a direct conflict and inconsistency. The change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall not settle any third party claims against it regarding its use of any of the Franchisor Property without the prior written consent of Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: The Distributor shall not settle any third party claims against it regarding its use of any of the Franchisor Property without the prior written consent of Franchisor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Franchisee' to 'The Distributor' introduces uncertainty regarding settlement authority. The agreement consistently refers to the 'Franchisee' as having specific responsibilities and rights. By changing it to 'The Distributor' in this section, it's unclear whether this entity is a separate party or whether the 'Franchisee' is also acting as a distributor, thus leading to questions regarding settlement authority and potential liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnity. The Franchisee shall indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from all claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses in relation to or arising out of the breach of any representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be without prejudice to, and independent of any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, none of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby. The provisions of this Section 7.3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the 'Franchisee' requires prior written consent from the Franchisor to settle third-party claims. The changed text replaces 'Franchisee' with 'The Distributor'. The contradicted text states that the 'Franchisee' will indemnify the Franchisor against claims. If 'The Distributor' is not the same as the 'Franchisee', then the changed text creates a contradiction because it's unclear who is responsible for indemnifying the Franchisor, leading to uncertainty about which entity has settlement authority and associated liabilities. This falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because it uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
